zh.lifeSummary: 當Otto I. von Northeim Herzog von Bayern於1015年出生在德國海因里希_普洛文次 韓諾福諾特海姆時，他的父親Benno von Northeim20歲，母親Eilika26歲。他和Richenza Gräfin von Werl-Northeim至少有3個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1083年1月11日在德國尼德薩辛藍德可萊斯 格廷根韓諾福爾-姆爾登布爾斯費爾德享年68歲。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Northeim
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farmsteads named from nord ‘north’ + heim ‘home, farmstead’.

variants.name: Nordrum
meanings: Norwegian: habitational name from any of the five farms in eastern Norway, named in Old Norse as Northrheimr (see Nordheim ).

variants.name: Norheim
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Nels, Oddvar.
Norwegian: either a variant of Nordheim or from a farm name composed of Old Norse nór ‘narrows’ (see Nohr 1) + heimr ‘homestead, farmstead’.

nameMeanings.name: Otto
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a short form of any of the various Germanic compound personal names containing the element od, ot ‘prosperity, wealth’ ( compare the corresponding Old English ēad in names such as Edward and Edwin ). St Otto of Bamberg ( d. 1139 ) was a missionary to the Pomeranians. Otto the Great ( 912–73 ) is generally regarded as the founder of the Holy Roman Empire, and the name has been borne by several members of German and Austrian royal houses. It was recorded occasionally among immigrants to England from the Low Countries in the 14th century, but failed to establish itself. Since the 19th century it has again been used occasionally in the English-speaking world, mostly among immigrants from Germanic countries.

en.lifeSummary: When Otto I. von Northeim Herzog von Bayern was born in 1015, in Northeim, Hanover, Prussia, Germany, his father, Benno von Northeim, was 20 and his mother, Eilika, was 26. He had at least 3 sons and 3 daughters with Richenza Gräfin von Werl-Northeim. He died on 11 January 1083, in Bursfelde, Hannoversch-Münden, Göttingen, Lower Saxony, Germany, at the age of 68.

Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles avec Richenza Gräfin von Werl-Northeim. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie con Richenza Gräfin von Werl-Northeim. ko.lifeSummary: Otto I. von Northeim Herzog von Bayern(이)가 1015년 독일 프레우ss 프로빈츠 해노버 노르테임에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Benno von Northeim(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Eilika(은)는 26세였다. 그는 Richenza Gräfin von Werl-Northeim(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1083년 1월 11일 독일 니더작센 랜드크레이스 그o틴겐 해노버슈-u든 버스펠드에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Otto I. von Northeim Herzog von Bayern родился в 1015 г. в местности нортим, провинз ханновир, Пруссия, Германия, его отцу по имени Benno von Northeim было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Eilika, 26. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери от Richenza Gräfin von Werl-Northeim. Он умер 11 января 1083 г. в местности бурсфелд, ханновирщ-мuнден, ландкрис гoттинген, ниедирсачсен, Германия в возрасте 68 лет.
